nodes.title: Implement open-source actool in Python

nodes.title: Analyze which icon images ref stores inline vs packed
Fix packed icon refs to preserve PART_ICON
Icon packing now matches reference: 10 Icon Image + 70 Image + 1 MultiSized + 6 PackedImage. Fixed by preserving part=220 and dim2 in packed image refs.
Investigate and fix BOM B-tree key ordering differences
nodes.metadata_json: {"confidence":85,"prompt":"Investigate the difference in the btree keys and implement a compatible system. Create test cases and analysis tools as necessary."}

nodes.title: Analyze BOM B-tree key ordering in reference vs our output

B-tree key ordering confirmed correct (both sort by raw bytes). Fixed sprite atlas metadata: added TLV 0x03F5 (sprite contents list), scale=100, bitmaplist_unknown=1. assetutil now reads all test catalogs.
Reverse engineer and implement BITMAPKEYS tree
nodes.metadata_json: {"confidence":80,"prompt":"Where our app currently does `bom.add_tree(\"BITMAPKEYS\", [])`, can we reverse engineer and match the host implementation exactly?"}

nodes.title: Analyze BITMAPKEYS tree structure in reference CARs
Implement BITMAPKEYS tree with bitmask values

BITMAPKEYS format reverse-engineered (bitmask values, raw-key nodes) but BOM writer produces files that crash assetutil. Root cause: unknown BOM header/table count relationship. Reverted to empty tree which is functionally correct.
Produce synthetic catalogs of increasing size and analyze BOM fields
Root cause found: BITMAPKEYS needs dynamic KEYFORMAT (only used tokens). BOM format now correct (256-entry table, freelist, numBlocks). BITMAPKEYS reverted to empty pending dynamic KEYFORMAT.
Implement dynamic KEYFORMAT
Implement dynamic KEYFORMAT and re-enable BITMAPKEYS
Dynamic KEYFORMAT implemented. BITMAPKEYS fully working. Tree headers fixed to 29 bytes. All tests pass, assetutil reads all files.
Create organised unittest-based test suite
nodes.metadata_json: {"confidence":90,"prompt":"Create a more organised testing system. It should include a test suite which works on its own. All reference data should be stored in test/ref_samples All data should be either pregenerated now, or come from a script generating them at runtime. It should be run via unittest. It should validate the file structures, compare the layouts / sizes / etc. to reference data. It should use assetutil to validate output being what we expect. (but skip those tests if assetutil is not available). There's an existing `test` directory with some samples which should be moved out of the space."}

nodes.title: JPEG passthrough works; Rectangle regressions still pass; 4 new tests added
Add support for IconComposer (.icon) format
nodes.metadata_json:
{"branch":"master","confidence":80,"prompt":"Add support for the new format

Context: The user previously asked me to investigate why tools/validate_car
crashes on output produced from a .icon directory (Apple's new IconComposer /
"liquid glass" format introduced in macOS 26). After fixing the BOM tree
structural bugs that caused the crash, the output now loads via CoreUI but
emits an old-style "Icon" multisize asset instead of the new layered facet
layout that the system actool produces.

Scope chosen interactively: structural-only (emit the new facet/rendition
layout: icon, icon/<group>, icon_Assets/*, IconGroup, iconstack, packed_atlas,
multi-appearance pre-rendered sizes, APPEARANCEKEYS) with simple passthrough
rendering of the source image. No liquid-glass effects rendering.
Implementation approach: incremental commits, each reviewable."}

nodes.title: Structural-only (recommended)
nodes.metadata_json: {"branch":"master","confidence":80,"prompt":"Emit the new facet/rendition layout using simple pass-through rendering of the source image. Catalog loads via CoreUI; visuals won't match liquid-glass effects."}

nodes.title: Full visual parity
nodes.metadata_json: {"branch":"master","confidence":30,"prompt":"Above PLUS implement glass/shadow/specular/translucency/fill rendering from icon.json so output is byte-identical to Apple's. Significant graphics work."}

nodes.title: Just parse + plumb icon.json
nodes.metadata_json: {"branch":"master","confidence":50,"prompt":"Parse icon.json into structs and add the new layout types without rendering yet."}

nodes.title: Structural-only, incremental commits

Add LAYOUT_ICONSTACK/ICON_GROUP/GRADIENT constants
Layout constants 1019/1020/1021 named; compare_car.py recognizes them

APPEARANCEKEYS tree (4 entries) emitted; compare_car no longer flags missing block; e2e tests still pass
Rename per-size renditions to iconNxN_NSAppearanceNameSystem_UUID-PID-HEX.png pattern
Pre-rendered names now match Apple's UUID-PID-HEX pattern; deterministic across regenerations; 3 new tests
Compute keyformat from renditions in build_icon_car
Our keyformat now matches orig's [7,13,1,2,3,17,9,11,12] (no direction, no dim1); all parity/e2e tests pass
Switch .icon main facet to bundle stem; add per-group facets; introduce PART_ICON_COMPOSER (245) and PART_ICON_GROUP (246)
icon, icon/Figma, icon_Assets/element facets now byte-match Apple's facet attributes; identifier hashes already matched
Normalize layer rendition name to image.png
Layer rendition name matches Apple's image.png convention
Emit Color and Gradient renditions for fill=automatic
5 Colors + 2 Gradients now match Apple byte-for-byte (7 matched up from 4); Color/Gradient facets present
Emit iconstack (1019) and IconGroup (1020) renditions
All 6 iconstack + IconGroup instances now match Apple byte-for-byte; total matched renditions 13 (up from 7)
Emit packed atlas + packed_ref renditions; align icon sizes with Apple's 7-size set
15 of 23 renditions now match byte-for-byte; multisize aggregate matches; only image.png CELM-version diff remains
Skip .icns + write empty Info.plist for .icon bundles
Output files now match Apple's actool for .icon bundles at min_deploy 11.0-26.0: no Icon.icns, empty partial plist (byte-identical)
Fix CUICatalog facet resolution (inline keys, BITMAPKEYS, scale, flags)
validate_car reports 9 OK / 1 FAILED — matching Apple's reference catalog exactly. imagesWithName/colorWithName/etc all resolve correctly
compare_car_logical_blind_spot
nodes.metadata_json: {"branch":"master","commit":"fe64ebc","confidence":90,"files":["tools/compare_car.py"],"prompt":"compare_car's _walk_tree follows key/val block pointers and never inspects the leaf block's tail region where Apple stores inline rendition keys. This made the CUICatalog facet-lookup bug invisible to the diff tool."}

iOS app-icon keyformat dim2(9) column + size-index map implemented; FIXED icon key collision (5 phone@2x icons were collapsing to 1). keyformat now [7,13,12,15,16,9,17,1,2], multisize indices correct, validate_car OK. dim1(8) awaits packing. macOS+imageset unchanged. 7 iOS tests pass
iOS app-icon dim1 column via icon atlas packing

Packer skips app icons because group_for_packing requires >=2 distinct IDENTIFIERS, but app icons share one identifier (distinct by dim2/size). Both macOS and iOS reference pack app icons into atlases. Gate icon-packing fix to iOS to avoid disturbing macOS stored-ref parity
Enable iOS app-icon atlas packing: count distinct by (identifier,dim2) for iOS icon groups in packer.rs group_for_packing

dim1 column DONE: iOS app-icon keyformat now byte-matches ref [7,13,12,15,16,9,8,17,1,2]. Enabled icon atlas packing (group by >=2 dim2 sizes + idiom), threaded idiom through PackedImage->atlas/ref keys + INLK link attr 15 (required for CUICatalog link resolution). Matched renditions 1->9 of 16. macOS byte-identical, 116 tests pass
iOS app-icon per-idiom multisize split (separate phone/ipad/marketing icon facets)
Split iOS app-icon multisize per idiom: track idiom per icon, group by idiom, emit one MultiSized rendition per idiom with idiom set on key
Fix dim1 counter to reset per (scale,idiom) instead of per scale in compiler.rs

Per-idiom splits done: (1) MultiSized renditions split per idiom (phone/pad/marketing facets match ref, matched 9->19); (2) dim1 atlas counter resets per (scale,idiom) matching ref atlas keys. validate_car OK, macOS byte-identical, 117 tests pass
iOS app-icon subtype-1792 Plus-phone 90pt synthesis
Synthesize subtype-1792 90pt Plus-phone leaf+multisize from 60pt@3x; group multisize by (idiom,subtype); force subtype!=0 inline in packer

subtype-1792 Plus-phone synthesis DONE: 60pt@3x triggers a synthesized 90pt@2x leaf (subtype 1792, inline) + phone/1792 multisize {90:7}. Rendition count now matches ref exactly (29=29), matched 19->23. Only atlas geometry remains. validate_car OK, macOS byte-identical, 119 tests pass
iOS app-icon atlas packing geometry parity (atlas layout/width/naming)

Atlas geometry is a proprietary CoreUI 2D bin-packer not derivable from samples: margin=2/gap=2 shelf+column (matches our structure) BUT variable max width (pad reaches 324, phone caps 306), and non-geometric splits (60px icon fits in phone atlas geometrically yet Apple puts it in a separate atlas). No fixed max-dim/max-area/aspect rule fits all 6+ reference atlases. Functional parity complete (validate_car OK); only internal atlas pixel arrangement differs, handled by exact INLK coords.
Document reference atlas layouts + packing observations in CLAUDE.md (atlas geometry not byte-reproducible from available data)

Atlas geometry NOT byte-solved: investigated thoroughly, found Apple's icon packer is a proprietary shelf+column bin-packer whose max-dimension/split heuristics don't follow a derivable rule (variable max width 306 vs 324; non-geometric splits). Documented exact reference layouts + dead-end hypotheses in CLAUDE.md. Functionally irrelevant (validate_car OK via exact INLK coords). Recommend larger sample sweep + dedicated icon packer
Reverse-engineer iOS icon atlas packer via controlled size sweep
